Video.summary: A Meddling Mother-in-Law or a Lying Cheating Daughter-in-Law? Dr Phil delves into the family drama.
Video.title: Toxic and Offensive Inlaws

Video.summary: When you imagine getting an inheritance, will your financial problems be solved or can the money be more trouble than it’s worth?
Video.title: Fighting Over the Will

Video.summary: Parents say their daughter's home is unsafe for grandson.
Video.title: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Image.alt: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Video.summary: From spending $600,000 on 3-year-old beauty queens, to pushy stage sisters and an ambitious boxing coach — today's guests say they go to the extremes to help the youngest members of their family excel. Meet two fathers, one whose full-time job is managing his tiny stars' careers, and the other who hand makes each and every one of his 4-year-old daughter's costumes. Are these dads bonding with their daughters or simply pushing too hard? Plus, meet several women who defiantly criticize child beauty pageants. And, hear from one overzealous sister and the father of a boxing champion once ranked first in the world. Can Dr. Phil convince these guests to step aside and strive for balance in their children's lives?
Video.title: Competing For The Title

Video.summary: Parents express frustration with their twin sons' disrespectful behavior.
Video.title: Big Time Moochers!

Video.summary: A divorced couple secretly remarries, but did they make a mistake- again?
Video.title: Make It or Break It

Video.summary: Brenda says she fell in love with and married her husband, Don – despite their 26-year age gap. But soon after, she claims her “Prince Charming” turned “crazy” and “temperamental.” Is their age difference at the root of their issues? Then, Bev says she’s concerned for her sister, Melanie, who married her much younger man, Devar, after meeting him on a Jamaican vacation. Is Devar in it for love, or for a green card and money? And, Dr. Phil catches up with best-selling author Mitch Albom.
Video.title: Regret Marrying Older Spouse

Video.summary: Dr. Phil takes goes inside the unthinkable: making your own child sick to fulfill a disturbing need for attention. The idea might sound unimaginable, but for people with a psychological disorder called Munchausen by Proxy, it's irresistible. Note: Viewer discretion is advised.
Video.title: Munchausen Mom

Video.summary: Danielle says that her husband of nearly 10 years, John, has been living a secret life -- reaching out to men on Craigslist for sex and looking at child pornography. John insists that the child porn was accidentally downloaded, and while he admits to exchanging emails with men online, he maintains that he never met up with anyone in person. How does he explain his actions? And, what will the results of a polygraph test reveal?
Video.title: My Husband’s Secret Life

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his look at a teacher sex scandal that has a school and families divided in a small town in Texas. High school teacher Joelle Ogletree faces accusations of sexual misconduct with some of her male students. Hear both sides of this gripping sex scandal from the accuser and the accused, and judge for yourself if justice has been served.
Video.title: Teacher Sex Scandal (PT. 2)

Video.summary: Em says her husband, Shawn, has cheated on her with at least 78 women -- so why does she keep taking him back? Shawn admits to at least 20 affairs but says it’s his wife’s fault that he strays.
Video.title: Why Men Cheat

Video.summary: A racist man tries to change his ways. A man finds out he is biracial.
Video.title: Racism Follow Up

Video.summary: Did a woman accused of fake pregnancies really lie?
Video.title: Baby Scam? Amie's Explosive Reply

Video.summary: Amanda Knox spent four years locked in an Italian prison for the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Dubbed “Foxy Knoxy” by the press, she became one of the most famous cases of wrongful conviction. She tells her story, as she fights to save the life of Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed. This program contains graphic discussion about a criminal case. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Amanda Knox: Freeing The Innocent

Video.summary: The conversation continues with a woman who says her sister lied about health diagnoses for attention.
Video.title: Saving Shemida: What’s The Truth?

Video.summary: The contentious 2020 presidential election has shown that there’s a clear divide in America – half the country is celebrating, and the other half is angry – making the upcoming holiday season especially tricky to navigate. Instead of being a time of joy, celebration and giving thanks, many families are faced with the dilemma of how not to argue with their own spouses, children and parents about politics. Dr. Phil meets a Republican father, who is barely speaking to his Democrat daughter after an argument they had over Donald Trump. Then, Dr. Phil meets another father, a lifelong Democrat, who says he raised his son to be a free thinker ... but never imagined that he’d support Trump! The election is officially over, but the drama remains. What’s Dr. Phil’s advice on how to move forward post- election without turning holiday dinners with family into a war zone over politics? Tune in!
Video.title: 2020 Election: Families Divided

Video.summary: Couples who say their marriage is on the rocks due to debt.
Video.title: Marriage Crisis: Drowning in Debt

Video.summary: Terrell Owens is a six-time NFL pro-bowler and arguably one of the best wide receivers to play the game, but he may be better known for his antics off the field. His tumultuous career has been well-documented, and he's been called arrogant, toxic and friendless. The football great sits down with Dr. Phil to set the record straight about the bad rap he says he’s been getting, especially from the mothers of three of his children, who claim he's also a deadbeat dad who is behind on child support payments and makes little attempt to see his kids. What does Terrell think of their accusations? See what happens when the moms come together to confront the alleged absentee dad on Dr. Phil's stage about missed birthday parties, ignored phone calls and empty promises. Is Terrell living in the lap of luxury while his kids go without? What kind of relationship does he want with his children? Dr. Phil delves into Terrell's past to help him create a better future for his children. Plus, at 38, will he play in the NFL again?
Video.title: A Football Star’s Daddy Drama

Video.summary: The conversation continues with a self-admitted adult bully.
Video.title: Bullies Beware Part 2

Video.summary: Marcy and Scott say they believe their daughter, Danielle, has Stockholm syndrome from her boyfriend, Justin. They claim Justin is an abusive narcissist who has manipulated their daughter into believing he’s “God’s gift to the world.” Danielle says she loves Justin, and he’s not going anywhere. She says the only reason Justin has ever laid hands on her is to restrain her from her own angry rages, and her parents don’t understand their relationship. Justin says he’s a great guy with an “X factor” and doesn’t understand why Danielle’s parents dislike him. Although he’s currently dealing with a serious legal problem, he says he’s not a criminal, narcissist, or an abuser. And, in a shocking twist, Danielle reveals to Dr.Phil and her parents a secret about her relationship with Justin.
Video.title: Hula Hooper's Bad Boyfriend

Video.summary: Rylee, 26, admits she’s a sugar baby and says she has no shame in dating wealthy, married men she meets online in exchange for money and lavish gifts -- and three years ago, she even convinced her mother, Tanya, to join her. Dr. Phil sits down with this mother/daughter sugar baby team, who insist they never have sex with their clients. What “services” do they say these men are paying for? And how does their family feel about their lifestyle? Then, Ashley admits she has had a side job as a sugar baby for years but has yet to tell her fiancé, Cesar -- and she wants Dr. Phil to help her confess. How will Cesar respond? And, reality TV star Kendra Wilkinson opens up about her husband, Hank Baskett’s, alleged affair with a transsexual. Does she believe the rumors? And, can her marriage survive the turmoil?
Video.title: The Secret World of Sugar Babies

Image.alt: The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies
The Secret World of Sugar Babies

Video.summary: Secrets are revealed in a family with a shopaholic wife and rageaholic husband.
Video.title: Family Secrets, Family Lies

Video.summary: Parents, listen up! Did you know that 94 percent of American kids ages 12 to 17 use the Internet, and 58 percent of them have an online profile? We're living in a digital world, where photos and other content can be published online for the world to see in an instant. You may not know it, but your child may be one click away from disaster.
Video.title: Busted Online

Video.summary: Melanie says her 19-year-old daughter's boyfriend, Michael, is a gay, drug abusing gangbanger. What would cause her to have such a negative opinion of him? And is there any truth to her allegations? Learn why Michael initially agrees to a drug test, and then refuses. Will he finally break down and take it? And why does Melanie’s daughter, Caylin, support him anyway, regardless of the outcome? Then, Lori says her 17-year-old daughter, Mackenzie, is obsessed with a boy who's already broken up with her four times. Find out why Dr. Phil has a stern message for this mom.
Video.title: Hands Off My Teen Daughter

Video.summary: Baronica and Nathan say that on July 10, 2021, their then 9-year-old son shot his 8-year-old brother in the chest with one of six unsecured guns in their home. The parents bravely come forward to tell their story in hopes that other parents will learn from their mistakes. Then, Kristin says the last time she saw her 15-year-old son, Ethan, he was heading to his best friend’s house. Within hours, she was informed Ethan had been killed by an unsecured gun in his best friend’s house. Kristin has fought to have Ethan’s Law enacted in CT, which ensures that parents are held accountable for the safe storage of their weapons. And, hear from Susan, whose daughter Amanda was sitting on a park bench when she was shot by a group of young people, leaving her paralyzed. Plus, Ryan Busse, a former gun industry executive who says he left the industry after seeing how fear tactics were being used to sell guns, joins the conversation. This is a show every parent needs to watch -- whether you own a firearm or not.
Video.title: Unsecured Guns: Who’s to Blame?

Video.summary: Jessica says she was instantly attracted to Jason, known as “Tank D.,” one of the stars of CMT’s hit show Redneck Island, and they started dating. But now, Jessica claims the man she loved for over 18 months is an arrogant, self-centered narcissist who cares more about partying and verbally trashing her than their 6-month-old baby son. Jessica says she believes this reality show "celebrity" is not only a deadbeat dad but an abusive, manipulating narcissist who is acting inappropriately with his female fans and firmly believes he cheated on her with Katie, a co-star from Redneck Island. Jason says Jessica knew he was wild from the start and failed to tame his bad boy ways. He admits he’s called Jessica names but adamantly denies he’s ever been physically abusive toward her. Jason says Jessica needs to stop using their son as a manipulative tool, because they are not together. Jason 100 percent denies cheating with Katie on season 5 of Redneck Island, but Katie says otherwise -- and in a shocking revelation, Katie reveals the full truth to Jessica. Will Jason continue to deny the affair or finally admit to cheating?
Video.title: Reality TV Baby Daddy Drama

Video.summary: More couples get divorced in the first year of marriage than any other. Dr. Phil talks with newlyweds in crisis about having realistic expectancies and re-negotiating within the relationship.
Video.title: Was Our Marriage a Mistake?

Video.summary: Pedophiles once lurked in parks and around schoolyards, and then they prowled the playground of the World Wide Web. Now these predators have a more sinister method at their fingertips sending explicit text messages directly to your child's cell phone. Dr. Phil examines the latest technological threats to your kids and shows you how to protect them.
Video.title: Pursuing the Predator

Video.summary: Amie says she has no idea how to control her 7-year-old son, Jayden, and even began posting videos of his outbursts online seeking help. But Jayden’s father, Chad, says Amie is just out for attention and blames her for their son’s problems. Can these exes learn to co-parent peacefully?
Video.title: His Ex Posts Son’s Tantrums

Video.summary: The saga of a father's quest for his vanished daughter is discussed.
Video.title: Was Mommy Murdered?

Video.summary: The conversation continues with D’Andre, who blames his abusive relationship with his ex-girlfriend, Autumn, on her upbringing.
Video.title: Dad Confronts Daughter’s Abuser (Pt 2)
